sourceMovie.title: Nightmares
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Nightmares works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 28 Weeks Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Nightmares clicked for you, 28 Weeks Later (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Nightmares?
faq.answer: Try 28 Weeks Later, The Host, Black Box—each shares DNA with Nightmares while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Nightmares?
faq.answer: Nightmares (1983) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Nightmares worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
